title: How to Choose the Perfect Sofa for Your Living Room
excerpt: From frame and foam to fabric and proportions — a practical, designer-approved guide to buying a sofa you'll love for a decade.
author: Priya Menon
content:
Choosing a sofa is one of the most important furniture decisions you'll make. It anchors your living room, sets the tone for the space, and — if chosen well — serves you faithfully for ten years or more.

## 1. Start with the frame
The frame is everything. Look for kiln-dried hardwood such as sheesham or engineered hardwood. A good frame won't creak, sag or wobble after a few months. At Himalayan Furniture Mart, every sofa frame is kiln-dried and joint-reinforced.

## 2. Understand the foam
High-resilience (HR) foam holds its shape far longer than standard foam. For the seat, look for a density of 32–40 kg/m³. Back cushions can use a softer feather or fibre blend for that sink-in feel.

## 3. Measure your space — twice
Before you fall in love with a sofa, measure your room, your doorways and your lift. A sofa that won't fit through the door is a costly mistake. Leave at least 45 cm of walking space around it.

## 4. Choose the right fabric
Performance weaves resist stains and fading; boucle adds texture; leatherette is easy to wipe clean. Match the fabric to your lifestyle — homes with kids and pets benefit from tighter, stain-resistant weaves.

## 5. Think about proportion
A large room can carry an L-shaped sectional; a compact apartment is better served by a 2- or 3-seater. The sofa should feel generous but not crowd the room.

## The Himalayan promise
Every sofa we craft is built around the principle of *luxury look, everyday comfort* — premium materials, expert craftsmanship and a five-year frame warranty, delivered and installed across India.

title: 10 Space-Saving Furniture Ideas for Small Apartments
excerpt: Compact living doesn't mean compromise. These ten ideas help you maximise every square foot in style.
author: Ananya Reddy
content:
Living in a compact apartment is an opportunity for clever, intentional design. Here are ten ideas to make a small space feel expansive.

## 1. Choose multi-functional pieces
Storage beds, nesting tables and ottomans with hidden compartments earn their keep twice over.

## 2. Go vertical
Tall bookshelves and wall-mounted TV units draw the eye upward and free the floor.

## 3. Pick legs over skirts
Furniture on slim legs lets light flow underneath, making rooms feel airier.

## 4. Embrace floating furniture
Wall-mounted desks and TV consoles keep sightlines open and floors clear.

## 5. Use a consistent palette
Warm, light tones — like our Warm Ivory backdrop — visually expand a room.

The remaining ideas — mirrors, modular seating, foldable dining, dual-purpose study tables and slim wardrobes — all share one principle: every piece should work hard and look effortless.

title: The Complete Guide to Caring for Wooden Furniture
excerpt: Keep your solid-wood furniture looking new for decades with these simple care rituals.
author: Rajesh Kumar
content:
Solid-wood furniture is an investment that rewards a little care. Follow these rituals and your pieces will age beautifully.

## Dust weekly
Use a soft, dry microfibre cloth. Dust is abrasive over time and dulls the finish.

## Keep away from direct sun and heat
Prolonged direct sunlight fades wood; radiators and vents dry it out and can cause cracking.

## Manage humidity
Wood expands and contracts with humidity. Keep rooms reasonably stable to prevent warping — our kiln-dried timber handles this well, but extremes are best avoided.

## Clean spills immediately
Blot, don't rub. For sticky residue, use a barely-damp cloth and dry immediately.

## Polish occasionally
A quality wood polish every few months nourishes the finish. Avoid silicone-heavy sprays that build up residue.

With these habits, your Himalayan furniture will look as good in ten years as it does today.

title: Designing a Home Office That Boosts Productivity
excerpt: Ergonomics, lighting and the right desk setup — how to build a workspace you actually want to work in.
author: Arjun Nair
content:
A great home office is equal parts ergonomics and atmosphere. Here's how to get both right.

## Start with the chair
You'll spend hours in it, so invest here first. A mesh ergonomic chair with lumbar support and synchro-tilt keeps you comfortable and focused.

## Get the desk height right
Your elbows should rest at ~90°. A desk height of 73–76 cm suits most people; the Summit executive desk is built to this standard.

## Light it well
Position your desk perpendicular to a window to avoid glare, and add a warm task lamp for evening work.

## Cable management matters
A clutter-free desk is a clutter-free mind. Choose furniture with built-in cable routing.

## Add one beautiful thing
A plant, a piece of art or a warm wood finish makes the space feel inviting — and you'll show up more often.

title: Warm Minimalism: The Decor Trend Redefining Indian Homes
excerpt: Less clutter, more warmth. How earthy tones and natural materials are shaping the modern Indian home.
author: Priya Menon
content:
Warm minimalism is the antidote to cold, clinical interiors. It keeps the calm of minimalism while adding the soul of natural materials and earthy colour.

## The palette
Think warm ivory, soft beige, terracotta and walnut — exactly the tones we build our collections around. They create rooms that feel serene yet alive.

## Natural materials
Solid wood, boucle, linen and stone bring texture and authenticity. They age gracefully and feel wonderful to live with.

## Intentional pieces
Buy fewer, better things. One beautiful sofa beats three forgettable ones.

## Negative space
Let your rooms breathe. Whitespace isn't empty — it's what makes the pieces you love stand out.

Warm minimalism isn't a trend so much as a return to timeless principles: quality, comfort and quiet beauty.

title: Is Custom Furniture Worth It? An Honest Breakdown
excerpt: When bespoke beats off-the-shelf — and when it doesn't. A candid look at custom furniture value.
author: Rajesh Kumar
content:
Custom furniture has a reputation for being expensive. The truth is more nuanced — and often pleasantly surprising.

## When custom wins
- **Awkward spaces:** alcoves, slanted walls and tight corners are where bespoke shines.
- **Exact storage needs:** a wardrobe built around your wardrobe wastes nothing.
- **A specific look:** when nothing off-the-shelf matches your vision.

## When ready-made is smarter
- **Standard rooms and budgets:** our catalogue covers most needs beautifully.
- **Tight timelines:** custom takes 4–6 weeks; stocked items ship faster.

## The Himalayan approach
We keep custom accessible — *high quality, low cost, no compromise*. Free design consultation, transparent pricing and a workshop that treats every commission as a signature piece.

Request a quote and we'll tell you honestly whether custom or catalogue is right for you.
